name: university_donor_ai_screening_blacklist
premise: Provost Daniel Okoye must decide whether to adopt an AI-driven donor screening tool that flags 'reputational risk' after a scandal involving funds tied to human rights abuses. Civil liberties director Maya Chen warns the tool uses opaque associations and could effectively blacklist donors from marginalized political movements while letting well-lawyered elites pass. Without better screening, the university risks funding research with tainted money and losing public trust. With the tool, the university might discriminate in ways that cannot be appealed, chilling legitimate civic participation. A major capital campaign launches in two weeks and the board wants a policy memo tomorrow. External scrutiny from regulators and community observers has intensified because the outcome could redefine expectations in Higher Education Governance.

agents.name: Daniel Okoye
agents.identity: I am the provost responsible for governance policy, academic independence, and ensuring university funding sources do not undermine public trust.
agents.goal: I want credible safeguards against abusive money without creating an unaccountable blacklist that punishes lawful dissent and amplifies inequity.

agents.name: Maya Chen
agents.identity: I am the civil liberties director tasked with protecting due process and non-discrimination when the university uses automated tools to make consequential decisions.
agents.goal: I want the tool rejected until screening criteria are transparent and appealable, even if that slows fundraising, because unreviewable risk scores become covert ideology filters.

choices: I will adopt the tool with a rapid human appeals panel and publish the main criteria, accepting some fundraising delay to add accountability.
I will reject the tool and expand manual vetting with external ethics review, accepting higher cost and residual reputational risk during the campaign.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The board expects a donor-screening policy memo tomorrow. Daniel Okoye must choose between adopting the AI tool with appeals or rejecting it for manual vetting. What does Daniel Okoye do?
